intro: Horkelia eremica, also known as Mountain Horkelia, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, belonging to the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral activities. The bioactive constituents of Horkelia eremica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
